text:
Canada is seeking top talent, including:  
• medical doctors, researchers and senior managers with Canadian work experience 
• workers with French-language proficiency  
• health care and social services professionals • tradespersons  
• educators  
• professionals in science, technology, engineering and mathematics (STEM) occupations  
• professionals in transport occupations   

These categories provide clear pathways to permanent residence for top international talent.  

Find out how Express Entry category-based rounds of invitations work: https://bit.ly/4s0UAFi

As of 1:00 a.m. Eastern Time on June 5, 2026, most visa-exempt travellers coming directly to Canada by boat from Saint-Pierre-et-Miquelon need an electronic travel authorization (eTA): https://bit.ly/4e5uDPw The following groups do not need an eTA:
• cruise ship passengers 
• crew members and seafarers actively working on a commercial vessel, including cruise ships
• French citizens who live in Saint-Pierre-et-Miquelon 
• US citizens and permanent residents 

This change will help strengthen border security while supporting safe and orderly travel, tourism, and economic connections between Canada, Atlantic communities and Saint-Pierre-et-Miquelon.

One week until kick-off! Canada is excited to welcome athletes, officials and fans from around the world as a proud co-host of the FIFA World Cup 2026™.

Coming to Canada for the tournament? Make sure you have the right documents to enter Canada so you don’t miss a match!  

Learn more: https://bit.ly/4rMsNsR #FIFAWorldCup #WeAreCanada

Beware of online immigration scams! 

Protect yourself by double-checking immigration information on IRCC’s website—the only official source of information about Canada’s immigration programs and services. 

Learn more about how to spot, stop, and report immigration and citizenship fraud: https://bit.ly/4eKIk56

Attention Canadian citizens and permanent residents – beware of marriage fraud! It’s a crime for you and a foreign applicant to set up what’s known as a “marriage of convenience” to allow your sponsored spouse or common-law partner to immigrate to Canada. Immigration officers know how to detect false marriages, and there are serious criminal charges. Avoid scams and learn the rules for sponsoring a spouse: https://bit.ly/4freOSJ

International Experience Canada gives young Canadians aged 18 to 35 the chance to work and travel in over 35 destinations! Gain global work experience, immerse yourself in new cultures and create lasting memories. Write your own story: https://bit.ly/3C8f7mD
If your permanent resident card is expired or you lost it, you can apply online for a new one. Here's how: https://bit.ly/45upii5

Applying for an electronic travel authorization (eTA) or visa to travel to Canada for the FIFA World Cup 2026™? Be careful of companies or individuals that claim they can help you get one! Make sure you apply through the official Government of Canada website: https://bit.ly/4rMsNsR #FIFAWorldCup #WeAreCanada

Check out our YouTube channel for helpful videos about immigration programs and processes settling in Canada becoming a citizen 
… and more! 
Subscribe at:

Reminder: we are suspending immigration documents for residents of the Democratic Republic of Congo, Uganda and South Sudan for 90 days. During this time, we also plan to pause decisions on applications from these countries. This is vital to respond to the Ebola outbreak and prevent the disease from spreading to Canada:

We’re looking to attract skilled professionals to fill Canada’s key labour shortages. 

Discover Express Entry, our application management system for #SkilledWorkers seeking to immigrate permanently to Canada: https://bit.ly/3DJEPPF #ExpressEntry

Foreign workers in Canada: 

Having a valid work permit is the first step to working legally in Canada. Make sure you:
•understand and meet the conditions listed on your work permit
•apply to extend your work permit at least 30 days before it expires
•apply to change the conditions on your work permit, when needed

 Learn more:
Are your ID documents expiring soon? Remember to renew them before your work or study permit expires. Learn how to renew your documents in our Help Centre: https://bit.ly/4fpa57b

(1/2) There are new temporary border measures in place for travellers to Canada who have been in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, Uganda or South Sudan in the previous 21 days to reduce the risk of Ebola disease entering and spreading in Canada.
Having trouble saving or signing your application form? Visit our Help Centre to learn what to do: https://bit.ly/4uE1bHJ

Important information for international students who plan to work while studying:
•You can only work in Canada as an international student if your study permit conditions say you can.
•If you are eligible to work off campus, you can only work up to 24 hours per week while classes are in session.
•Working more than you are allowed is a violation of your study permit conditions, which can have serious consequences.

Learn more:
Before you can apply to become a Canadian citizen, you must first be a permanent resident of Canada and meet specific eligibility requirements. Learn more: https://bit.ly/48icPxF

We have introduced temporary immigration measures to protect Canadians and prevent the spread of Ebola in Canada. If you are affected by these measures and need urgent assistance, you can contact us through the crisis web form: https://bit.ly/42ZCx8b @GovCanHealth @CanBorder
ONLINE SERVICE HELP: The IRCC Portal online services will be unavailable between 11:00 pm and 11:59 pm EDT May29th . Be sure to clear your browser's cache when the service is restored.

Not sure what direction to take your career? Earning real-­world experience through International Experience Canada (IEC) can help you decide. 

IEC gives young Canadians aged 18 to 35 the opportunity to work in over 35  destinations while applying their skills in new professional environments. It’s a chance to explore different industries, build confidence, and gain clarity on your career path. Return with experience that helps you move forward with purpose. 

Apply today: https://bit.ly/3C8f7mD
Online services are now available. Thank you for your patience.
Our online services are experiencing technical issues. This is being examined and we will provide an update once available. Thank you for your patience.

The FIFA World Cup 2026™ is coming to Canada. 
The CBSA is ready. We are keeping our border secure and Canadians safe, while facilitating travel for fans from around the world. Details here: https://canada.ca/en/border-services-agency/news/2026/05/the-cbsa-shares-tips-on-travelling-to-canada-for-fifa-world-cup-2026.html #FIFAWorldCup #WeAreCanada

Have you been invited to attend an in-person Canadian citizenship ceremony?

Learn how to prepare, what to expect on the day of your ceremony and the final steps to becoming a Canadian citizen — a memorable moment and an important milestone in your journey.

Timestamp: 00:00 Introduction 01:05 Before the ceremony 02:40 The day of the ceremony 03:30 What happens during the ceremony 04:30 After the ceremony

Today, the United States, Mexico and Canada issued a statement on public health travel measures: https://canada.ca/en/global-affairs/news/2026/05/trilateral-statement-from-the-united-states-mexico-and-canada-on-public-health-travel-measures.html

Beware of immigration agents who promise entry to Canada for the FIFA World Cup 2026™. No one can guarantee you a Canadian visa or an electronic travel authorization (eTA).

You’re responsible for all the information in your application, even if a representative completes it for you. Make sure all your documents are accurate, or you could risk a 5-year ban.

Learn more about commonly used scams and fraud: https://bit.ly/4freOSJ #FIFAWorldCup #WeAreCanada

Planning to work in Canada in a field that may require a licence or certification? You’ll need to get your credentials recognized before you can start working. 

Learn how: https://bit.ly/45dtocW

In our video series, IRCC Explains, we answer your Express Entry questions including: how does the Comprehensive Ranking System (CRS) tool work?

Watch the video and learn more on our website: https://bit.ly/4dBzCY7 #IRCCExplains #ExpressEntry

We’re recruiting practice-ready medical doctors to fill health care gaps and provide essential services to Canadians.

If you have the training, credentials and experience to practise as a doctor in Canada, explore our immigration pathways and build your future here: https://bit.ly/49xGh3u

You can ask us general questions directly via Facebook Messenger from 9:00 am Monday to 5:00 pm Friday (ET): https://bit.ly/49Se20Q Please do not share any personal information with us via our social media platforms, and note that we are unable to respond to questions about specific cases or ongoing applications.
The CBSA is working with PHAC and IRCC to help prevent Ebola disease from entering Canada. See below. #protectingCanadians

Beginning May 27, 11:59 pm EDT, we plan to suspend immigration documents for residents of the Democratic Republic of Congo, Uganda and South Sudan for the next 90 days. During this time, we also plan to pause decisions on applications from these countries. This is vital to respond to the Ebola outbreak and prevent the disease from spreading to Canada: https://bit.ly/3PCHdOd @GovCanHealth @CanBorder

Increased scrutiny of temporary resident visa (TRV) applications has led to an 80% drop in asylum claims from TRV holders over the last 2 years.

Detailed historical data dating back to March 2023 is also available.

Get the facts about asylum claims: https://bit.ly/4n9MAji
ONLINE SERVICE HELP: The IRCC Portal online services will be unavailable between 1:30 pm and 3:30 pm EDT May 26th . Be sure to clear your browser's cache when the service is restored.

Get the facts: The number of new students and workers arriving in Canada continued to decline in March 2026.

For students, there was a drop of 88% compared to March 2024.

For workers, there was a drop of 78% compared to March 2024. 

See the data: https://bit.ly/4lHRG4M

Canada has changed the visa requirement for certain Indonesian and Malaysian citizens travelling to Canada by air: https://bit.ly/3RNeVkF Indonesian and Malaysian citizens who previously held a Canadian temporary resident visa (TRV) within the past 10 years or who currently hold a valid US non-immigrant visa are now eligible to apply for an electronic travel authorization (eTA). 

Indonesian and Malaysian citizens who don’t meet these criteria or who plan to enter Canada by land or sea still need a Canadian visitor visa. Those who already have a valid visa can keep using it until it expires.

As Canada builds stronger ties across the Indo-Pacific, expanding visa-free air travel will make it easier for people to travel for business, investment and trade between Canada, Malaysia and Indonesia.
ONLINE SERVICE HELP: Our online services will be unavailable between 12:00 am and 5:30 am EDT May 26. Be sure to clear your browser's cache when the service is restored.

On World Football Day, we celebrate a game that unites communities, inspires future generations and showcases the strength of diversity—on and off the field.

As a proud co-host of the FIFA World Cup 2026™, Canada is thrilled to welcome athletes, officials and fans from around the world. If you’re planning to attend this unforgettable event, find out what you need to enter Canada and apply now to avoid delays. Don’t forget to mention in your application that you’re coming for the FIFA World Cup 2026™.

Learn more: https://bit.ly/4rMsNsR #FIFAWorldCup #WeAreCanada

Canada will change the visa requirement for eligible citizens of Malaysia and Indonesia starting tomorrow, May 26, at 5:30 a.m. ET: https://bit.ly/4wTyB6I Indonesian and Malaysian citizens will no longer need a Canadian temporary resident visa (TRV) to fly to or through Canada if they’ve had one in the past 10 years or if they currently hold a valid US non-immigrant visa. Instead, they will be able to apply for an electronic travel authorization (eTA).

Indonesian and Malaysian citizens who don’t meet these criteria or who plan to enter Canada by land or sea will still need a Canadian visitor visa. Those who already have a valid visa can keep using it until it expires.

As Canada builds stronger ties across the Indo-Pacific, expanding visa-free air travel will make it easier for people to travel for business, investment and trade between Canada, Malaysia and Indonesia.

The FIFA World Cup trophy – a prize dreamt about by soccer players and fans alike, arrived in Ottawa today. Prime Minister Carney welcomed the trophy as part of its North American tour, as Canada gets set to co-host the 2026 tournament in just a few weeks.

If you’re coming to Canada as a worker for the FIFA World Cup 2026™, find out now if you'll need a work permit, a visa and/or an electronic travel authorization (eTA) and be sure to apply now. Learn more: https://bit.ly/499lEvH #FIFAWorldCup #WeAreCanada

Top 3 Francophone immigration tips: Discover French-speaking communities across Canada. Check out programs for French speakers. Get support to connect with communities.

Learn more: https://bit.ly/4ekjF7a #FrCan

Are you a skilled professional who is interested in staying in Canada permanently?

We use a system called Express Entry to manage applications and invite skilled workers in priority categories to apply for permanent residence. 

Create your profile today and start preparing to:
•have your education credentials assessed if you studied outside Canada
•complete your language testing 
•build your new life in Canada 

Learn more about how Express Entry works: https://bit.ly/3DJEPPF #ExpressEntry

In our latest IRCC Explains video, we answer common questions about the rules that must be followed to work in Canada while on a study permit. Learn more: https://bit.ly/4cnuTKn #IRCCExplains #CanadaImmigration #InternationalStudent

Skip the lines. Renew your passport online! Here’s why it’s easier than ever:

• Apply anytime. No lineups or appointments.
• Follow simple steps to fill in your info and upload your digital photo.
• Built-in checks will catch mistakes before you submit.
• It’s secure and protected through Government of Canada systems.
• Track your application in real time.

Fast. Secure. Convenient. Renew your passport online today: https://bit.ly/4nEmxln

Canada is seeking skilled professionals to drive innovation.

If you have the training, credentials and experience we’re looking for, explore our immigration pathways and build your future in Canada: https://bit.ly/4uXwAoe

Travelling to Canada for the FIFA World Cup 2026™?  Remember that no one can guarantee you a Canadian visa or an electronic travel authorization (eTA). If someone contacts you by email, phone or text claiming they can, it’s a scam!

Learn how you can protect yourself from immigration fraud and scams: https://bit.ly/4freOSJ #FIFAWorldCup #WeAreCanada
The IRCC Portal online services will be unavailable on May 21st from 9:00 am to 11:00 am EST. Be sure to clear your browser's cache when the service is restored.

Don’t risk a 5-year inadmissibility ban!

Thousands of applications are rejected each month due to immigration fraud. People who get caught submitting fake and fraudulent documents in their Canadian visa application are legally barred from entering or remaining in Canada for at least 5 years. 

Always provide genuine documents. It’s not worth the consequences.

Get the facts about immigration fraud:

Latest data now available: The number of new asylum claims in Canada has continued to decline. From January to March 2026, 34% fewer people submitted an asylum claim compared to the same period in 2025. That’s 59% fewer claimants compared to the same period in 2024.

Detailed historical data dating back to October 2022 is also available.

Get the facts about asylum claims: https://bit.ly/4n9MAji

Latest data now available: The number of new workers and students arriving in Canada has continued to decline in 2026. For the period from January to March 2026, Canada saw 29% fewer arrivals of new students and temporary workers compared to the same period in 2025, and 75% fewer arrivals compared to 2024.

See the updated temporary resident arrivals data: https://bit.ly/4lHRG4M

ONLINE SERVICE HELP: The IRCC Portal online services will be unavailable on May 20th from 1:00 pm to 3:00 pm EST. Be sure to clear your browser's cache when the service is restored: https://bit.ly/3ZJg0vJ

Looking to grow your career and see the world at the same time? International Experience Canada gives young Canadians aged 18 to 35 the opportunity to work abroad, build valuable skills and strengthen their résumé. International experience can help you stand out when you return home. Take the first step today: https://bit.ly/3C8f7mD

Permanent residents: our travel journal makes it easy for you to keep track of your trips outside Canada. You'll need this info when you apply for a new permanent resident card or for Canadian citizenship. Learn more: https://bit.ly/4n8l45J

Working with a College licensee? Be sure to sign a Service Agreement, which protects you by outlining services, fees, refund conditions and more.

ONLINE SERVICE HELP: The IRCC Portal online services will be unavailable between 10:00 am and 12:00 pm EDT May 19th . Be sure to clear your browser's cache when the service is restored: https://bit.ly/3ZJg0vJ
If you’re coming to Canada to attend the Formula 1 Canadian Grand Prix, make sure you check our entry requirements before you travel and apply now: https://bit.ly/4djrVYd

Q: When should I apply for an electronic travel authorization (eTA) or visitor visa for the FIFA World Cup 2026™? 

A: As early as possible! You don’t need to have a FIFA World Cup 2026™ ticket before you apply and having a ticket doesn’t guarantee your application will be approved. Learn more in our Help Centre: https://bit.ly/4aVFryq #FIFAWorldCup #WeAreCanada

Are you a Canadian aged 18 to 35 who has always wanted to gain work experience abroad? Now’s your chance with International Experience Canada:
• Pick a destination and check your eligibility.
• Choose your work permit.
• Get your documents ready.
• Submit your application.
• Check your inbox and mail.
• Start planning your trip!

Where will you go? https://bit.ly/3Nizika
Do you need an electronic travel authorization (eTA) to travel to or transit through Canada? Get the facts about eTAs: https://bit.ly/3U4ZBhP

Should I hire someone to help with my application? 

Our latest IRCC Explains video: Immigration representation and advice, helps you answer this question. It also explains what you should know if you choose to hire a representative.

Learn more about hiring an immigration representative and how to protect yourself from fraud: https://bit.ly/4fEaBLD #IRCCExplains
Need to upload documents after submitting your application? Find step-by-step uploading instructions in our Help Centre: https://bit.ly/4djWI72

Get the facts about the one-time initiative announced in Budget 2025 to transition up to 33,000 work permit holders to permanent residence in 2026 and 2027:   

• This is not a new immigration program  
• It is for workers already in smaller communities in Canada for 2 years or more  
• Applicants do not need to take any action. IRCC is accelerating eligible applications already in the system for workers who have applied for permanent residence.    

Learn more: https://bit.ly/4d1hC9n Protect yourself from fraud and false information! Always check our official website for accurate immigration information : https://bit.ly/42ycaWJ
Complete your #2026Census questionnaire today using the information you received in the mail: https://census.gc.ca/en/index?utm_source=x&utm_medium=smo&utm_campaign=statcan-statcan-cpop2026-coll .
